sourceMovie.title: The Menu
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Menu works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The 'Burbs
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Menu clicked for you, The 'Burbs (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Psycho II
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Menu clicked for you, Psycho II (1983) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Menu?
faq.answer: Try The 'Burbs, Psycho II, Wrath of Man—each shares DNA with The Menu while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Menu?
faq.answer: The Menu (2022) is best described as Comedy, Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Menu worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
